ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_listings" (
  "listing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job listing */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job position */,
  "salary_estimate" TEXT /* Estimated salary range provided by Glassdoor */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job responsibilities */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the hiring company */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the job is based */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Headquarters of the company */,
  "size" TEXT /* Size of the company (employee count) */,
  "founded" INTEGER /* Year the company was founded */,
  "ownership_type" TEXT /* Type of ownership (e.g., Private, Government) */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the job position */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector associated with the industry */,
  "revenue" TEXT /* Estimated revenue of the company */,
  "competitors" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of competitors */,
  "hourly" INTEGER /* Indicates if the salary is hourly (1) or not (0) */,
  "employer_provided" INTEGER /* Indicates if the salary is provided by the employer (1) or not (0) */,
  "min_salary" INTEGER /* Minimum salary estimate */,
  "max_salary" INTEGER /* Maximum salary estimate */,
  "avg_salary" REAL /* Average salary calculated from min and max */,
  "company_txt" TEXT /* Short description of the company */,
  "job_state" TEXT /* State where the job is located */,
  "same_state" INTEGER /* Indicates if the job is in the same state as the user (1 or 0) */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the job listing in days */,
  "python_yn" INTEGER /* Indicates if knowledge of Python is required (1) or not (0) */,
  "R_yn" INTEGER /* Indicates if knowledge of R is required (1) or not (0) */,
  "spark" INTEGER /* Indicates if knowledge of Spark is required (1) or not (0) */,
  "aws" INTEGER /* Indicates if knowledge of AWS is required (1) or not (0) */,
  "excel" INTEGER /* Indicates if knowledge of Excel is required (1) or not (0) */,
  "job_simplifier" TEXT /* Simplified job title for matching purposes */,
  "seniority" TEXT /* Seniority level of the job */,
  "desc_len" INTEGER /* Length of the job description in characters */,
  "competitors_count" INTEGER /* Count of competitors in the same industry */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for creation */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for last update */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the companies table */,
  "job_type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the job_types table */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the industries table */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the sectors table */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the locations table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("listing_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Headquarters of the company */,
  "size" TEXT /* Size of the company (employee count) */,
  "founded" INTEGER /* Year the company was founded */,
  "ownership_type" TEXT /* Type of ownership (e.g., Private, Government) */,
  "revenue" TEXT /* Estimated revenue of the company */,
  "description" TEXT /* Short description of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_types" (
  "job_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job type */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of job (e.g., Full-time, Part-time, Internship) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the location */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Recruiter, Analyst, Job Seeker) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_job_listings" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user job listing */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "listing_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the job_listings table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_benchmarking" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary benchmarking entry */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job position */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the job is based */,
  "avg_salary" REAL /* Average salary for the job position */,
  "min_salary" REAL /* Minimum salary for the job position */,
  "max_salary" REAL /* Maximum salary for the job position */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trend_analysis" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend analysis entry */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job position */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the job is based */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Growth rate of the job position */,
  "demand" REAL /* Demand for the job position */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
